migration_examples.description: Migration of FROptionsBuilder (legacy) to the modern Journey builder syntax, including the move of OAuth properties into a dedicated Oidc module.
migration_examples.notes: The legacy 'timeout' and 'oauthSignOutRedirectUri' properties appear to be optional or handled differently in the new simplified structure. The new structure often includes a 'logger' and may require a 'discoveryEndpoint' for OIDC, which was implicit or inferred in the old SDK.

key_mapping_summary.notes: Moved into the Oidc configuration module.
Moved into the Oidc configuration module.
Must be converted from a space-separated string to a mutableSetOf strings. New SDK version often defaults to more scopes.
The authServiceName is often used as a separate parameter during the 'authenticate' call, or can be set as a top-level 'serviceName' property.

migration_examples.description: Migration from FRSession.authenticate with NodeListener callbacks to the modern Journey.start() with sealed class pattern for handling different node states.
migration_examples.notes: The legacy SDK used callback-based NodeListener pattern with separate methods for success, exception, and callback received events. The new SDK uses a more idiomatic Kotlin approach with sealed classes (ContinueNode, SuccessNode, FailureNode, ErrorNode) that can be handled with when expressions, providing better type safety and flow control.

key_mapping_summary.notes: Method signature simplified. Context parameter no longer needed. Returns a Node directly instead of using callbacks.
Callbacks are now represented as ContinueNode in the sealed class hierarchy. Use pattern matching to handle.
Success state is now represented as SuccessNode. Session information available through the node properties.
Exceptions are now represented as ErrorNode with error details accessible through node properties.
New explicit state for authentication failures (different from errors/exceptions).

migration_examples.description: Migration of TextInputCallback from legacy ForgeRock SDK to the new Ping Identity SDK, showing how user input is handled and mapped.
migration_examples.notes: Legacy SDK used explicit TextInputCallback class and required manual extraction of prompt and input. The new SDK uses a more idiomatic Kotlin sealed class (TextInputCallback) and property access, simplifying usage.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(TextInputCallback::class.java)
val prompt = callback.getPrompt()
callback.setText("user input")

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as TextInputCallback
val prompt = callback.prompt
callback.text = "user input"

key_mapping_summary.notes: Type casting is more idiomatic in Kotlin.
Property access instead of getter method.
Property assignment instead of setter method.

migration_examples.description: Migration of PasswordCallback from legacy SDK to new SDK with property-based access.
migration_examples.notes: The new SDK uses property assignment instead of setter methods for password values.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(PasswordCallback::class.java)
val prompt = callback.getPrompt()
callback.setPassword(password.toCharArray())

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as PasswordCallback
val prompt = callback.prompt
callback.password = password.toCharArray()

key_mapping_summary.notes: Type casting is more idiomatic in Kotlin.
Property access instead of getter method.
Property assignment instead of setter method.

migration_examples.description: Migration of NameCallback for username/name input from legacy to new SDK.
migration_examples.notes: NameCallback follows the same property-based pattern as other text input callbacks.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(NameCallback::class.java)
val prompt = callback.getPrompt()
callback.setName("username")

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as NameCallback
val prompt = callback.prompt
callback.name = "username"

migration_examples.description: Migration of ChoiceCallback for single or multiple selection from a list of choices.
migration_examples.notes: The new SDK maintains similar structure but uses property access. Choice arrays and default selections are accessed via properties.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(ChoiceCallback::class.java)
val choices = callback.getChoices()
val defaultChoice = callback.getDefaultChoice()
callback.setSelectedIndex(0)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as ChoiceCallback
val choices = callback.choices
val defaultChoice = callback.defaultChoice
callback.selectedIndex = 0

migration_examples.description: Migration of ConfirmationCallback for user confirmation actions (Yes/No, OK/Cancel, etc.).
migration_examples.notes: ConfirmationCallback provides options for user confirmation. The new SDK uses property access for options and selected index.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(ConfirmationCallback::class.java)
val options = callback.getOptions()
callback.setSelectedIndex(0)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as ConfirmationCallback
val options = callback.options
callback.selectedIndex = 0

migration_examples.description: Migration of TextOutputCallback for displaying information to the user.
migration_examples.notes: TextOutputCallback is read-only and displays messages. The new SDK uses property access.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(TextOutputCallback::class.java)
val message = callback.getMessage()
val messageType = callback.getMessageType()

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as TextOutputCallback
val message = callback.message
val messageType = callback.messageType

key_mapping_summary.notes: Property access for message type (INFO, WARNING, ERROR).

migration_examples.description: Migration of PollingWaitCallback for handling asynchronous operations with polling.
migration_examples.notes: PollingWaitCallback indicates the client should wait before proceeding. The new SDK uses property access for wait time.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(PollingWaitCallback::class.java)
val waitTime = callback.getWaitTime()
val message = callback.getMessage()

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as PollingWaitCallback
val waitTime = callback.waitTime
val message = callback.message

key_mapping_summary.notes: Property access for wait time in milliseconds.

migration_examples.description: Migration of KbaCreateCallback for creating Knowledge-Based Authentication questions and answers.
migration_examples.notes: KbaCreateCallback allows users to create security questions. The new SDK uses property access and simplified question selection.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(KbaCreateCallback::class.java)
val predefinedQuestions = callback.getPredefinedQuestions()
callback.setSelectedQuestion("What is your favorite color?")
callback.setSelectedAnswer("Blue")

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as KbaCreateCallback
val predefinedQuestions = callback.predefinedQuestions
callback.selectedQuestion = "What is your favorite color?"
callback.selectedAnswer = "Blue"

key_mapping_summary.notes: Property access for predefined questions list.

migration_examples.description: Migration of TermsAndConditionsCallback for accepting terms and conditions.
migration_examples.notes: TermsAndConditionsCallback requires user acceptance. The new SDK uses property access for terms text and acceptance status.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(TermsAndConditionsCallback::class.java)
val terms = callback.getTerms()
val version = callback.getVersion()
callback.setAccept(true)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as TermsAndConditionCallback
val terms = callback.terms
val version = callback.version
callback.accept = true

migration_examples.description: Migration of BooleanAttributeInputCallback for boolean attribute collection.
migration_examples.notes: BooleanAttributeInputCallback is used for collecting boolean attributes. The new SDK uses property access.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(BooleanAttributeInputCallback::class.java)
val name = callback.getName()
val value = callback.getValue()
callback.setValue(true)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as BooleanAttributeInputCallback
val name = callback.name
val value = callback.value
callback.value = true

migration_examples.description: Migration of StringAttributeInputCallback for string attribute collection.
migration_examples.notes: StringAttributeInputCallback collects string-based attributes. The new SDK uses property access.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(StringAttributeInputCallback::class.java)
val name = callback.getName()
val prompt = callback.getPrompt()
callback.setValue("value")

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as StringAttributeInputCallback
val name = callback.name
val prompt = callback.prompt
callback.value = "value"

migration_examples.description: Migration of NumberAttributeInputCallback for numeric attribute collection.
migration_examples.notes: NumberAttributeInputCallback collects numeric attributes. The new SDK uses property access.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(NumberAttributeInputCallback::class.java)
val name = callback.getName()
val prompt = callback.getPrompt()
callback.setValue(42.0)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as NumberAttributeInputCallback
val name = callback.name
val prompt = callback.prompt
callback.value = 42.0

migration_examples.description: Migration of SelectIdPCallback for selecting an identity provider (social login).
migration_examples.notes: SelectIdPCallback allows users to select from multiple identity providers. The new SDK is in the external-idp module with property access.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(SelectIdPCallback::class.java)
val providers = callback.getProviders()
val provider = providers[0]
callback.setValue(provider.provider)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as SelectIdpCallback
val providers = callback.providers
val provider = providers[0]
callback.value = provider

migration_examples.description: Migration of IdPCallback for external identity provider authentication.
migration_examples.notes: IdPCallback handles OAuth/OIDC flows with external providers. The new SDK is in the external-idp module with suspend function support.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(IdPCallback::class.java)
val provider = callback.getProvider()
val tokenType = callback.getTokenType()
callback.setToken(token)
callback.setTokenType("id_token")

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as IdpCallback
val provider = callback.provider
val tokenType = callback.tokenType
callback.signIn(context, idpHandler)

key_mapping_summary.notes: Simplified with signIn suspend function that handles the flow.

migration_examples.description: Migration of DeviceBindingCallback for device binding and authentication.
migration_examples.notes: DeviceBindingCallback binds a device for authentication. The new SDK is in the mfa/binding module with suspend function support.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(DeviceBindingCallback::class.java)
val userId = callback.getUserId()
val challenge = callback.getChallenge()
callback.bind(context, fragmentActivity)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as DeviceBindingCallback
val userId = callback.userId
val challenge = callback.challenge
callback.bind()

key_mapping_summary.notes: Simplified bind() suspend function without context parameter.

migration_examples.description: Migration of DeviceSigningVerifierCallback for verifying device signatures.
migration_examples.notes: DeviceSigningVerifierCallback verifies device signatures. The new SDK is in the mfa/binding module with suspend function support.

legacy_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.getCallback(DeviceSigningVerifierCallback::class.java)
val userId = callback.getUserId()
val challenge = callback.getChallenge()
callback.sign(context, fragmentActivity)

new_interface.snippet:
val callback = node.callback as DeviceSigningVerifierCallback
val userId = callback.userId
val challenge = callback.challenge
callback.sign()

key_mapping_summary.notes: Simplified sign() suspend function without context parameter.

migration_examples.description: Migration of WebAuthnRegistrationCallback for FIDO2/WebAuthn registration.
migration_examples.notes: WebAuthnRegistrationCallback handles FIDO2 registration. The new SDK is in the mfa/fido module as FidoRegistrationCallback with suspend function support.

key_mapping_summary.notes: Simplified register() suspend function with exception handling.
Callback renamed and moved to mfa/fido module.

migration_examples.description: Migration of WebAuthnAuthenticationCallback for FIDO2/WebAuthn authentication.
migration_examples.notes: WebAuthnAuthenticationCallback handles FIDO2 authentication. The new SDK is in the mfa/fido module as FidoAuthenticationCallback with suspend function support.

key_mapping_summary.notes: Simplified authenticate() suspend function with exception handling.
Callback renamed and moved to mfa/fido module.

migration_examples.description: Migration of PingOneProtectInitializeCallback for initializing PingOne Protect.
migration_examples.notes: PingOneProtectInitializeCallback initializes Protect SDK. The new SDK is in the protect module with suspend function support.

key_mapping_summary.notes: Renamed to initialize() with suspend function.

migration_examples.description: Migration of PingOneProtectEvaluationCallback for evaluating risk with PingOne Protect.
migration_examples.notes: PingOneProtectEvaluationCallback evaluates risk signals. The new SDK is in the protect module with suspend function support.

key_mapping_summary.notes: Renamed to evaluate() with suspend function.
